reason:
Both answers converge on the same correct recommendation and hit the key points (contractor to stabilize/migrate, move to managed K8s, avoid recreating single point of failure via one hire, note on consolidation). Correctness is comparable between them.

The differentiator is clarity of writing. Answer A is full of broken grammar and awkward phrasing ("Hiring take 2-4 month," "eat one full head," "Trade obvious," "if team still bleed time") that hurts readability for a busy decision-maker. Answer B delivers the same substance in clean, precise prose, and also adds a useful caution against overreacting into a full replatform (ECS/Cloud Run/Nomad) during the coverage gap. Answer A's extra caveats (compliance/on-prem, exotic CNI) are a modest plus but don't offset its readability issues.

Given comparable correctness and coverage, B's clarity and concision make it the stronger answer overall.
